title: BEM-923 worker review
objective:
Create file governance/audit/internal/results/BEM923_WORKER_SYSTEM_PROTOCOL_V3_PROVIDER_MODEL_REVIEW.md with content # BEM-923 WORKER SYSTEM PROTOCOL V3 PROVIDER MODEL REVIEW
Status: WORKER_REVIEW_READY
Decision: APPROVED_WITH_NOTES
Findings:
- Operator correction is valid. BEM-921 described logical role mechanics but missed the provider execution layer.
- The full working protocol must include logical role, provider role, activation mechanism, exchange file, event log and proof for every step.
- Primary internal contour must be represented as GPT Analyst, Claude Auditor and Claude Executor.
- Reserve contour must replace Claude Auditor/Executor with GPT-Codex/Codex when Claude limits or availability prevent use.
- The analyst role may remain GPT in both primary and reserve unless a specific future policy changes it.
- The prompt describes role behavior. The provider policy decides which provider executes the role at runtime.
- GitHub Actions/codex-runner/webhook are mechanisms, not roles. They must write trace/proof/event records and carry provider_id and logical_role in envelopes.
- The internal contour algorithm from BEM-921 remains correct but must be enriched with provider mapping at every transition.
Required registries:
- governance/state/providers_registry.json: provider IDs, provider type, capabilities, limits, status.
- governance/state/contour_provider_policy.json: primary/fallback provider per logical role and contour.
- governance/state/provider_failover_policy.json: conditions and recording rules for provider switching.
- governance/state/elements_registry.json and governance/state/contours_registry.json must include provider policy refs.
Required envelope fields:
- logical_role, provider_id, provider_mode, contour_id, fallback_used, fallback_reason, runner_ref, event_ref, proof_ref.
Required roadmap update:
- BEM-920: elements/contours/prompts plus provider registry skeleton.
- BEM-921: proof hardening and non-null CI/Git SHA.
- BEM-922: Telegram input schema and GD routing.
- BEM-923: operator production gate.
- BEM-924: dispatch lifecycle with provider-aware auto-trigger.
- BEM-925: provider-aware object/contour execution lifecycle.
- BEM-926: managed channel/event/report pipeline with provider fields.
- BEM-927: E2E selftest including primary and reserve provider scenarios.
- BEM-928: Claude re-audit package with provider evidence.
Status recommendation:
- SYSTEM_PROTOCOL_V3_PROVIDER_MODEL_APPROVED_WITH_REQUIREMENTS.
No issue comments.
